Accountants.reason: Routine accounting, reconciliation and financial reporting are being automated by AI accounting software widely adopted in Singapore's SME sector. ICPAS acknowledges significant role transformation.

Financial Analysts and Related Professio.reason: Bloomberg and MAS-regulated broker research already integrate AI-generated analysis. Singapore capital markets face significant compression as LLMs generate earnings models at near-zero cost.

PORTERS, ATTENDANTS AND RELATED WORKERS.reason: Physical handling of goods, luggage and equipment requires human presence at Singapore's hotels, ports and public venues. PWM wage floors protect this role.

Bakers, Pastry and Confectionery Makers.reason: Artisanal baking requires physical skill, dough handling and oven management. Automation exists at industrial scale but Singapore's F&B sector relies on skilled human bakers. F&B PWM applies.

Building Painters and Related Workers.reason: On-site physical painting, surface preparation and finishing require human dexterity and on-the-spot judgment. BCA site safety regulations require human workers.

Air Traffic Controllers.reason: CAAS-certified safety-critical role requiring real-time human judgment. Full automation is not legally permissible in Singapore's airspace management framework.

Security Guards.reason: PLRD-licensed security in Singapore is covered under PWM. AI surveillance systems take on monitoring functions, but human guards remain required for physical intervention and emergency response.

Physicists and Astronomers.reason: Research scientists at A*STAR and Singapore universities use AI for data analysis and simulation, but scientific creativity, experimental design and peer-review accountability remain human.

Fashion and Related Models.reason: Modelling requires physical presence, body movement and live client interaction. AI-generated fashion imagery exists but does not substitute for live model work in Singapore's events and retail sectors.

ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT RELATED TRADES WORK.reason: Physical electrical trade work requires EMA certification and on-site installation. AI diagnostic tools assist but cannot perform hands-on trade work.

Stationary Plant and Machine Operators N.reason: Automated plant systems are widely deployed in Singapore's manufacturing sector. Human operators shift to monitoring and exception handling roles.
Automated plant systems are widely deployed in Singapore's manufacturing sector. Human operators shift to monitoring and exception handling roles.

Customs and Immigration Officers.reason: ICA-issued authority and physical border presence are constitutionally required. AI assists screening but officers retain full decision-making authority.

LEGISLATORS AND SENIOR OFFICIALS.reason: Constitutionally elected or appointed roles in Singapore Parliament and Ministries. Political authority, public accountability and constituency representation cannot be delegated to AI systems under Singapore's system of parliamentary democracy.

Religious Professionals.reason: Pastoral care, spiritual guidance and religious ritual require human presence and authentic emotional connection. Singapore's diverse faith communities depend on human religious leadership for community cohesion.

Poultry Farm Workers.reason: Animal husbandry requires physical care, feeding and monitoring of live animals. Singapore's local food security farms rely on hands-on workers. Some automation in feeding exists but cannot fully replace farm worker presence.

LIBRARIANS, ARCHIVISTS AND CURATORS.reason: Cataloguing and search are increasingly automated by AI systems in NLB and national archives. However, curation, community outreach and archival interpretation require human expertise and cultural judgment.

Civil Engineering Technicians.reason: Site supervision, material inspection and BCA compliance checks require on-site human presence. AI assists with drawings and calculations but field technician roles remain essential in Singapore's construction sector.
